input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

### Xcode (for iOS)
- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (currently Xcode 16.x).
- After installing, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- This gives you iOS SDKs, simulators, and Apple build tooling.

### Android Studio (for Android)
- **Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- During setup, ensure you install:
  - **Android SDK** (latest stable API level)
  - **Android SDK Build-Tools**
  - **Android SDK Command-line Tools**
  - **Android Emulator** + a system image for testing
- Set the `ANDROID_HOME` env var (typically `~/Library/Android/sdk`):
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$PATH"
  ```
  Add these to `~/.zshrc` (or `~/.bashrc`).

### JDK (required by Android tooling)
- .NET MAUI bundles **Microsoft OpenJDK** automatically via the workload installer (see below), so you typically don't need a separate install.

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) or via Homebrew.
- Install the **latest stable .NET SDK** (currently .NET 9):
  ```bash
  # Option A: Official installer from dot.net
  # Option B: Homebrew
  brew install --cask dotnet-sdk
  ```
- **Discovering the right version:** MAUI ships as a workload on the latest .NET release. Check [github.com/dotnet/maui](https://github.com/dotnet/maui) or `dotnet --list-sdks` for compatibility.

---

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in all sub-workloads: `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `maui-maccatalyst`, plus the bundled OpenJDK and Android SDK components if missing.

**Discover available workloads:**
```bash
dotnet workload search maui
```

**Check installed workloads:**
```bash
dotnet workload list
```

---

## 4. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + Extensions Marketplace |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued) | No longer supported — use VS Code or JetBrains Rider |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

For VS Code, install the **C# Dev Kit** and **.NET MAUI** extensions.

---

## 5. Verify Everything

```bash
# .NET SDK
dotnet --version
dotnet --list-sdks

# MAUI workload
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui"

# Xcode
xcode-select -p               # should print /Applications/Xcode.app/...
xcrun simctl list devices     # lists available iOS simulators

# Android
$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list
$ANDROID_HOME/emulator/emulator -list-avds

# End-to-end: create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # iOS build (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android    # Android build
```

If `dotnet build` succeeds for both target frameworks, your environment is fully working.

---

## 6. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `xcode-select: error` | Re-run `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Android SDK not found | Verify `ANDROID_HOME` is set and contains `platforms/` |
| `workload maui not installed` | Run `sudo dotnet workload install maui` again; check `dotnet workload repair` |
| iOS sim won't boot | Open Xcode → Settings → Platforms → download a simulator runtime |
| Java errors during Android build | Run `dotnet workload install maui` (it bundles OpenJDK); or set `JAVA_HOME` to a JDK 17+ |

That covers the full path from a clean macOS to building MAUI apps for both platforms.
